rationale: A complete WMI event subscription persistence triad was created by Administrator on host-01: an EventFilter (EV-001) triggering on notepad.exe process start, a CommandLineEventConsumer (A0) executing a heavily obfuscated PowerShell payload (-nop -w hidden -encodedcommand), and a Filter-to-Consumer binding (EV-002) linking them. DER-001 confirms the decoded payload decompresses a gzip-compressed blob via IEX, a classic in-memory execution pattern used to load second-stage malware without touching disk. The subscription was subsequently modified ~6.5 hours later (EV-003, EV-004) to change the trigger process to 'not-notepad.exe', indicating active operator interaction and refinement of the implant. While the payload's final decoded content is not available to confirm the downstream action, the combination of WMI persistence, no-profile hidden PowerShell, base64+gzip layered encoding, and IEX execution is strongly indicative of malicious post-exploitation activity with no plausible benign explanation.
